title: Capurodendron pervillei
intro: Capurodendron pervillei, also known as the Perville's tree or the Madagascar rosewood, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Capurodendron pervillei include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Capurodendron pervillei is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and is a part of the Sapindales order. The plant is also known as a member of the Capurodendron genus, which is part of the larger Sapindaceae family. Capurodendron pervillei is further classified within the family as part of the tribe Cardiaceae. Its classification is still being researched and refined for more precise understanding.
